data.title: Coinbase launches three new updates for agentic AI
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) on Thursday shipped three updates to span the agentic economy with specific tools for business, people, and developers.

"We believe agents are going to become the main way we access and navigate the internet across the board," the company said in a social media post. "This includes agents becoming the most active participants in the digital economy, operating on behalf of people 24/7, at a scale no human ever could."

The updates come after agentic traffic exceeded human traffic on Coinbase's (COIN) Base documentation pages for the first time last month. 

Beginning this week, Coinbase Business users will be able to accept USDC (USDC-USD) payments from AI agents. As part of the platform, businesses will also be able to manage, reconcile, and cash out agent payments in one account; earn 3.35% in rewards on idle USDC balances; and receive payments without chargeback risks.

For people, Coinbase for Agents will add real-time market views and conditional actions within guardrails set by the user.

For developers, Coinbase Developer Platform will let builders add x402 payment acceptance to any API, MCP server, or web service with three lines of code, the company said.

Coinbase (COIN) fell 2.1% in afternoon trading.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands gains as investors look past near-term headwinds
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands (LVS) went back into positive territory on Thursday after an initial post-earnings decline as analysts and investors looked past near-term headwinds.CBRE Equity Research analyst John Decree noted that an unusually low VIP hold in Macau negatively impacted EBITDA by $87M, which was only partially offset by favorable hold at Marina Bay Sands, adding $37M of EBITDA. He also noted that the World Cup held back traffic at some locations.The firm lowered FY27 EBITDA estimates on Buy-rated Las Vegas Sands (LVS) and cut its price target to $62 but still sees Las Vegas Sands (LVS) as uniquely attractive, trading at 7.5X the new FY27 EBITDA estimate. "Marina Bay Sands is arguably the most valuable casino asset in the world, with real long-term growth ahead with IR 2. Although LVS's recovery in Macau remains a show-me story, we are encouraged by the volume growth in Q2," highlighted DeCree.Macquarie also kept a bullish view on Las Vegas Sands (LVS) after the earnings release but clipped its price target. "We would view the underlying trends more constructively than the reported numbers suggest given market share gains across customer segments and strategic initiatives that we believe will bear fruit over time," updated analyst Chad Beynon.

"While near-term visibility remains imperfect, we continue to like LVS for its limited exposure to risks impacting other consumer sectors (prediction markets, gas price inputs, etc.), leverage to global wealth and experiential trends, and valuation that we believe does not fully reflect the durability of its Macau/Singapore franchises," added Beynon. Macquarie has an Outperform rating on LVS and a new price target of $62.00.Shares of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) were up 1.2% at 2:02 p.m.

data.title: Banner projects mid-single-digit 2026 loan growth while expecting Bank of the Pacific deal to close in Q3
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Banner Corporation (BANR) Q2 2026

Management View

  • "Banner Corporation reported a net profit available to common shareholders of $48.9 million or $1.43 per diluted share for the quarter ended June 30, 2026." (President, CEO & Director Mark J. Grescovich)

  • "Banner's second quarter 2026 revenue from core operations was $172 million compared to $163 million for the second quarter of 2025, an increase of nearly 6%." (President, CEO & Director Grescovich)

  • "We continue to benefit from a strong core deposit base that has proved to be resilient and loyal to Banner, a very good net interest margin and core expense control." (President, CEO & Director Grescovich)

  • "Core deposits continue to represent 89% of total deposits" and Banner "announced a core dividend of $0.52 per common share." (President, CEO & Director Grescovich)

  • "Loan originations were strong again this quarter" and "loan originations increased 45% when compared to the linked quarter, with commercial originations up 85%, construction up 73% and consumer up 55%." (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Jill Rice)

  • "We reported $1.43 per diluted share for the second quarter compared to $1.60 per diluted share for the prior quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Robert Butterfield)

Outlook

  • "Third quarter will probably come down a little bit in originations and loan growth, yet we still expect to end the year -- the full year at that mid-single-digit growth rate." (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice)

  • "Going forward, I would expect probably through the end of the year, we might see 1 to 2 basis points of increase quarter-over-quarter" in loan yields. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • "I'm expecting deposit costs to remain relatively flat" and "the CD book is pretty much fully repriced at this point." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • "A couple of basis points of margin expansion in the third quarter" was described as possible given deposit seasonality and reduced FHLB reliance, while "beyond that, it's going to be tougher." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

Financial Results

  • "Core pretax pre-provision income increased $1.9 million or 3% compared to the second quarter of last year" and "the decrease in earnings per share compared to the prior quarter was primarily driven by a higher provision for credit losses, lower noninterest income and higher noninterest expense, partially offset by stronger net interest income." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • "Net interest income increased $3.6 million from the prior quarter" and "the tax equivalent net interest margin was 4.13% compared to 4.11% in the prior quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • "Total noninterest income decreased $939,000 from the prior quarter" and "total noninterest expense increased $5.4 million from the prior quarter" including "$924,000 of nonrecurring expense related to the write-off of the previous commercial loan origination system." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • "The net provision for credit losses in the quarter was $3.8 million" and "loan losses in the quarter were modest, totaling $577,000 and were offset in part by recoveries totaling $476,000." (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice)

Q&A

  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler & Co.: Asked for new-loan pricing and the outlook for loan yields; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield: "the average yield on new loan production for the quarter was 6.53%" and "through the end of the year, we might see 1 to 2 basis points of increase quarter-over-quarter."

  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler & Co.: Asked about deposit spot rates, NIM in June, and deposit-cost direction; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield: "deposit costs were relatively flat throughout the quarter" and "I'm expecting deposit costs to remain relatively flat" while noting, "we have started to see CD specials in our marketplaces."

  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler & Co.: Pressed on expense run-rate and technology investments; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield: "if you back out the loan origination system, write off the old one, the M&A expense for the quarter, that's going to get you pretty close" and said the new system is contributing to "the pull-through and the timing on how quickly we can get loans through the pipeline."

  • Ryan Payne, D.A. Davidson: Asked if strong loan growth can continue amid payoffs; Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice: "we still expect to end the year -- the full year at that mid-single-digit growth rate" and "CRE payoffs are slowing, but they're not done."

  • Meghan Lynch, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods: Asked about capital return and buybacks during the Bank of the Pacific acquisition; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield: "we put any alternative capital actions outside of the core dividend on hold until we get the Bank of the Pacific deal closed" and "we expect it to close here in the third quarter."

  • Andrew Liesch, The Benchmark Company: Asked about funding mix and margin impact; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield: "we'd expect those FHLB advances to continue to come down" and agreed there could be "a couple of basis points of margin expansion in the third quarter."

  • Evan Kwiatkowski, Raymond James: Asked what drove broad loan growth and where opportunities are; Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice: "it really was both" and "it was broad-based" across markets, adding, "we don't have an industry or a particular geography that is doing all of the work for us."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Slightly negative analyst tone centered on margin durability, deposit competition, and expense levels, including direct challenges such as "Maybe speak to your thoughts on the run rate going forward" (Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler & Co.) and questions about competitive CDs and payoffs.

  • Slightly positive management tone in prepared remarks, with confidence anchored in operating performance and balance-sheet position, including "our capital and liquidity positions remain strong" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield) and "we remain well positioned to navigate the current environment" (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice).

  • Compared with Q1, management language in Q2 leaned more toward near-term headwinds from funding mix and costs (FHLB usage and higher expenses), while still maintaining guidance language such as "mid-single-digit growth rate" (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice) and reiterating expected seasonality benefits.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 profitability moved down from Q1, with "$1.43 per diluted share for the second quarter compared to $1.60 per diluted share for the prior quarter" and management tying the change to "a higher provision for credit losses" and "higher noninterest expense." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

  • Loan growth discussion shifted from Q1’s payoff-driven flat balances to Q2’s expansion, as Q2 included "loan outstandings grew by $287 million in the quarter" after Q1 noted loans "decreased $14 million" from year-end due to payoffs. (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice)

  • Capital return emphasis shifted from Q1 share repurchases ("Banner repurchased 250,000 shares") to Q2 pausing buybacks pending close of Bank of the Pacific ("put any alternative capital actions... on hold"). (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

Risks and Concerns

  • Credit risk monitoring focused on consumer strain and a specific construction credit, with "nonperforming assets increased by $8.9 million, the result of a single condo construction project moving to nonaccrual" and additional watchfulness on consumers where borrowers face "the strain that they're experiencing" in a higher-rate period. (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Rice)

  • Funding and pricing risk language included competition returning to CDs, as management said, "we have started to see CD specials in our marketplaces" and indicated holding deposit costs flat is the near-term objective. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Butterfield)

Final Takeaway

Banner’s management framed Q2 around resilient core funding ("core deposits... 89%"), strong loan production and broad-based pipelines, while acknowledging quarter-to-quarter earnings pressure from a larger provision, softer noninterest income, and a higher expense quarter that included a nonrecurring system write-off. Executives reiterated expectations to finish 2026 at a "mid-single-digit growth rate," pointed to possible third-quarter margin lift from seasonal deposit inflows and reduced wholesale funding, and emphasized that share repurchases are paused until the Bank of the Pacific acquisition closes in Q3.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Yelp giving ChatGPT access to customer reviews, business information -- report
data.description:
Yelp (YELP) is licensing its reviews, photos, and business information to OpenAI (OPENAI), giving ChatGPT access to richer business data when generating responses, according to Axios. 

Terms of the agreement were not disclosed, and the arrangement with ChatGPT does not limit Yelp (YELP) from making similar deals with competitors. Yelp (YELP) currently provides data to Yahoo+ (APO) and Apple (AAPL) Maps. A previous arrangement with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) eventually resulted in Yelp (YELP) suing Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) for prioritizing Google results over comparable results from Yelp.

Rather than develop a competing product, Yelp (YELP) will provide the information to the chatbot in return for the Yelp branding and appropriate links to the site to be included in the AI-generated response.

Yelp’s (YELP) “Request a Quote” feature will also be included eventually, enabling users to contact the merchant directly through ChatGPT.

The partnership gave Yelp (YELP) shares a shot in the arm into Thursday’s open, but that stock has since surrendered gains and is lower for a third straight day.

data.title: UBS raises United Rentals estimates after strong quarter eases margin concerns
data.description:
United Rentals (URI) drew a bullish response from UBS after posting stronger-than-expected second-quarter results, with the investment bank saying the equipment rental giant's execution and improving demand support further earnings growth.

UBS analyst Steven Fisher reiterated his Buy rating on United Rentals (URI) and raised his price target to $1,350 from its previous level of $1,300 after the company reported earnings that beat Wall Street expectations and lifted its full-year outlook. Fisher said investors had entered the quarter expecting a beat-and-raise, but lingering concerns over margins made the results more impressive.

"We think investors should be positively surprised by the margin growth and better than expected fleet productivity," Fisher wrote.

The analyst said United Rentals (URI) continues to benefit from growing activity on large non-residential construction projects while demand in local markets remains stable. He added that the company has been effective at putting more equipment to work as manufacturers meet requests for additional fleet, a trend he said should also benefit equipment suppliers.

Fleet productivity rose 3.4% during the quarter, comfortably ahead of the roughly 2.0% consensus expectation, driven in part by stronger-than-expected equipment utilization and higher rental rates, Fisher said. Rental gross margins also exceeded expectations, while a gain from the sale of scaffolding assets was recorded outside the rental business and therefore did not inflate margin performance.

For investors, the results reinforce expectations that spending on large industrial and infrastructure projects will accelerate during the second half of the year, supporting faster earnings growth for equipment rental companies.

Fisher also said margin trends could continue improving as fuel and delivery costs ease and the company completes restructuring initiatives that are expected to generate roughly $45 million to $50 million in annual savings. United Rentals (URI) reported about $12 million in restructuring benefits during the second quarter.

Following the earnings report, UBS increased its financial forecasts, raising revenue estimates for fiscal 2026 through 2028 by 2.4%, 4.0% and 5.2%, respectively. The firm also lifted its adjusted EBITDA estimates by 3.2%, 3.6% and 4.8% over the same period, citing stronger fleet utilization, moderating cost pressures and continued momentum in non-residential construction.

UBS now forecasts adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of $8.11 billion in 2026, $8.87 billion in 2027 and $9.58 billion in 2028.

Fisher's new $1,350 price target is based on a 10.5-times enterprise value-to-ebitda multiple, reflecting what he views as improving growth prospects and execution, even though the valuation sits above the stock's typical trading range over the past two years. He noted the shares have traded at similar multiples during previous periods of strong investor optimism.
